purpose: Persist the current implementation audit wave for the Codex Wizard operating system.

lane_registry.checked: CODEX.md, MANIFEST.json, v2 memory packet, and handoff
lane_registry.concluded: Implementation creates a clear contract but user-facing output must compress receipts and uncertainty.
lane_registry.open: Future auto-loading and dry-run proof remain unresolved.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md ownership, Waves 0-8, output surfaces, return-to-sims gate, and manifest v2 pointer.

lane_registry.checked: Codex vs Claude/Hermes/A2, Wizard vs replacement, voices vs LLM Council, Results vs Follow-up, proposed prompts vs receipts
lane_registry.concluded: Distinctions mostly survive; Wave 6 needs receipt truth to avoid blurring proposed prompts with ran work.
lane_registry.open: File-history proof is limited because wiki memory is outside a git repo.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md boundary, additive Wizard rule, separate Council wave, Results/Follow-up separation, v2 non-canon marker.

lane_registry.checked: JSON validation, voices, worker truth, triad, wave runtime, grouped follow-up, Results separation
lane_registry.concluded: Test plan mostly passed; explicit dry scenarios were missing before the follow-up patch.
lane_registry.open: Whether JSON validation should cover every repo JSON remains out of scope.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md stable voices, Waves 0-8, registry fields, output surfaces, and grouped options 1-18.

lane_registry.checked: CODEX.md clarity, stable labels, single-spaced follow-up language, and authority wording
lane_registry.concluded: CODEX.md is clear; the overbroad every-turn and every-follow-up language needs explicit receipt/defer handling.
lane_registry.open: Whether Wave 6 means spawned execution or bounded simulation needs per-turn receipts.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md root rule, Waves 0-8, registry fields, output surfaces, and All bundle lines.

lane_registry.checked: Independent Codex doc, v2 memory, manifest pointer, no Claude/Hermes/A2 authority, triad, council, follow-up waves, All bundles
lane_registry.concluded: Documented claims survive as operating contract; execution claims still require per-turn receipts.
lane_registry.open: Future turns need actual registry receipts to prove plurality.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md, MANIFEST.json, v2 memory packet, runtime_registry.json, and codex_harness_adapter references.

lane_registry.checked: Queue shape, worker cap truth, lane registry fields, stale-worker closure, handoff boundaries, return-to-sims leverage
lane_registry.concluded: Queue shape and boundaries are explicit; return-to-sims needs an operational trigger.
lane_registry.open: Configured max_depth is not empirically proven; stale closure should be receipted.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md Waves 0-8, registry fields, boundaries, return-to-sims gate, handoff, and config max_threads/max_depth.

lane_registry.checked: Codex Wizard against project purpose, process-drift prevention, return-to-sims gate, v5 process docs, controller contract, sim backlog, handoff
lane_registry.concluded: Wizard serves Codex Ratchet if treated as operating scaffolding, not project canon; main risk is ceremony drift.
lane_registry.open: No runtime proof that future turns auto-load CODEX.md and Codex memory.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md additive rule, triad duties, Waves 0-8, receipt truth, Codex boundaries, return-to-sims gate, and v5 sim backlog.

lane_registry.checked: Memory persistence, subagent incentives, follow-up execution, receipt truth, cognitive-load coupling, controller loops
lane_registry.concluded: The biggest system risk is load amplification; receipt truth is specified but needs enforcement surfaces.
lane_registry.open: Real runtime registry enforcement and Wave 6 execution mode remain open.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md full-wave requirements, registry rows, output surfaces, memory boundaries, and adaptive controller queue hygiene.

lane_registry.checked: CODEX.md, MANIFEST.json, and v2 memory for strongest objection
lane_registry.concluded: Strict implementation is blocked if every-turn/full-follow-up language is treated as fake execution instead of spawn/block/defer truth.
lane_registry.open: Patch should clarify registry/defer semantics without collapsing the user's all-attempt rule.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md every-turn rule and Wave 6 follow-up-run rule plus v2 repetition of every input/output attempt.

lane_registry.checked: Receipt truth, controller-local collapse, cross-system authority leaks, manifest/handoff boundaries
lane_registry.concluded: CODEX.md is mostly receipt-truth aligned and has no direct cross-system authority leak.
lane_registry.open: Broader manifest/handoff scans and future runtime receipts remain open.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md boundaries, registry rows, output surfaces, and reference-only status of archived/Hermes surfaces.

lane_registry.checked: Dirty surface, runtime registry, Codex control doc, harness integration status, key diffs, worker receipts
lane_registry.concluded: Closeable as this implementation turn after patching dry scenarios and receipt memory; not closeable as fully enforced runtime.
lane_registry.open: Depth proof, codex_harness_adapter validation, mini-MMM body verification, and dirty worktree reconciliation remain open.
lane_registry.evidence: runtime_registry, CODEX.md, codex_harness_adapter, live queue controller tests, harness integration status, and worker utilization receipt.

lane_registry.checked: CODEX.md, runtime_registry, .codex/mmm, codex-memory, skill registries, schema precedent, automation/proof artifacts
lane_registry.concluded: CODEX.md plus JSON memory is enough for guidance but not enforcement; typed receipt/admission schema is needed.
lane_registry.open: Where future Codex-owned schemas and runtime registry should live remains open.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md registry prose, .codex/mmm manifest and index, codex-memory manifest, JSON schema precedent, and skill registry precedent.

lane_registry.role: operating contract vs runtime harness framing audit
lane_registry.checked: Operating contract, memory packet, runtime harness, skill framing against CODEX.md and system_v5 harness docs
lane_registry.concluded: Best frame is runtime harness with operating-contract wrapper; memory alone risks storage without execution.
lane_registry.open: Stable versioned mini-MMM corpus location remains unresolved.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md plus system_v5 MMM harness, workflow boot architecture, and boot prompt templates.

lane_registry.checked: Auto-loading gap, markdown/no-markdown conflict, untracked files, follow-up explosion, runtime cap ambiguity
lane_registry.concluded: Codex Wizard is mostly documented contract, not fully enforced runtime; hidden risks are real but manageable with receipt/schema work.
lane_registry.open: Auto-loader, codex-memory backup, Wave 6 narrowing/receipting, and harness adapter role remain open.
lane_registry.evidence: CODEX.md Wave 0 and Wave 5/6 rules, imessage_bot formatting constraints, runtime_registry capacity caveats, codex_harness_adapter capacity caveats, git status.

survived: Codex now has an independent CODEX.md operating contract.
Codex memory v2 exists and the manifest points to it.
Claude/Hermes/A2 boundaries are explicit and were not made Codex authority.
Stable voices, Pushback, LLM Council, whole-context triad, 8-wave runtime, and follow-up make/run/audit are documented.
A typed lane registry schema and this receipt artifact now reduce pure-prose drift.
pushback_survived: The always-attempt rule can create overload or fake compliance unless registry/defer truth is explicit.
CODEX.md plus JSON memory is not full runtime enforcement; auto-load and validator integration remain open.
rejected: Do not claim future turns actually ran voices/subagents without per-turn receipts.
Do not treat follow-up menu options as completed work without Wave 6 receipts.
Do not import Claude/Hermes/A2 docs as Codex operating authority.
next_required: Run static checks for scenario and schema presence.
Keep final output compact and receipt-truthful.
